content:
Rats, cats, magpies Trying to define yourself online is a minefield. Even just picking a name feels impossible. Trying to find something that resonates with you is difficult, trying to find something that resonates but isn’t cringy is very difficult, and anything that fits that brief is guaranteed to be taken already. It’s a very annoying horrible venn diagram.
I almost always come back to animals that I identify with. Cats, rats, and birds all appealed to me. Yet all attempts to fit these animals into a name failed miserably (see afformentioned horrible venn diagram). That being said, I feel good with what I’ve settled on. The second line from the magpie nursery rhyme “One for sorrow, Two for joy”. Magpies are definitely in my top 3 birds of all time, and their tendency to pick up shiny things is an accurate parallel to how I pick up new hobbies. I like the idea of a two headed magpie, one head is more reserved, sensible, maybe a bit gloomy. The other head is more eclectic, hopeful, maybe lacking common sense. One head finishes WIPS, the other starts new ones. I’ll try an make a wee logo of them, it’ll be cute.
Yes, inside me are two wolves. Except they’re one magpie. The cringe levels are acceptable thank you.
summary:
Rats, cats, magpies

Trying to define yourself online is a minefield. Even just picking a name feels impossible. Trying to find something that resonates with you is difficult, trying to find something that resonates but isn’t cringy is very difficult, and anything that fits that brief is guaranteed to be taken already. It’s a very annoying horrible venn diagram.
I almost always come back to animals that I identify with. Cats, rats, and birds all appealed to me. Yet all attempts to fit these animals into a name failed miserably (see afformentioned horrible venn diagram). That being said, I feel good with what I’ve settled on. The second line from the magpie nursery rhyme “One for sorrow, Two for joy”. Magpies are definitely in my top 3 birds of all time, and their tendency to pick up shiny things is an accurate parallel to how I pick up new hobbies. I like the idea of a two headed magpie, one head is more reserved, sensible, maybe a bit gloomy. The other head is more eclectic, hopeful, maybe lacking common sense. One head finishes WIPS, the other starts new ones. I’ll try an make a wee logo of them, it’ll be cute.
title: Finding an identity for this blog (and me)

content:
Where else can I go? I’ve always found the wider social media landscape a bit intimidating. I was too young for MSN, never warmed up to facebook, snapchat, insta or whatever came after. I liked youtube, reddit, and discord, where I could enguage in hobby communities without wondering if my family would stumble across a comment I’d written. I never considered myself a heavy social media user.
And yet as we come to the halfway point of 2026, the UK faces wide sweeping changes to the social media world. A flat ban for under 16 year olds and, consequently, a requirement to prove that you’re over 16. Given I am not prepared to dish out my government ID to any backwater data broker on the net, I will be using a VPN for as long as that remains an option, and dipping out entirely otherwise. It made me think about how such an important commodity has come to rest in the hands of some of the greediest, most irresponsible people in our society. Big Tech holds the keys to so much of our lives, and our governments have done a historically shit job of protecting us from the greed and irresponsibility of these corporate giants.
So, I refer you to the video that showed me an alternative. As I write this, I’m struck by the barebones nature of the blog so far compared to the examples in the video, but hopefully that will begin to change!
The Indie Web I just missed out on this era of internet. It would never have occurred to me that the web could be traversed, site to site, link by link. But what a magical way to do it! If we want to shake off the taint of Google, of big tech as a whole, this seems like a lovely place to start. And hey maybe I’ll learn something about web dev along the way. God knows that native Android development as a carrer seems dead in the water.
It’s not perfect, I’ll be using GitHub to host, and will still be using sites like reddit, discord and youtube on a daily basis. But, it is a step in what feels like the right direction.
Keeping myself accountable This is straight forward. I have too many hobbies. True many of them intertwine and support each other, but that doesn’t make it any easier to stick to one project, finishing it and moving on. Crazy, impossible, it can’t be done sir. But, if I can use this blog to keep track of what I’m doing maybe it’ll be easier to keep some sort of mommentum, or at least not forget one of my several WIPs.
Supporting the artists I enjoy If nothing else, I hope someone else might stumble across this and appricate the links to other artists sites. These people amaze me.
summary:
Where else can I go?

I’ve always found the wider social media landscape a bit intimidating. I was too young for MSN, never warmed up to facebook, snapchat, insta or whatever came after. I liked youtube, reddit, and discord, where I could enguage in hobby communities without wondering if my family would stumble across a comment I’d written. I never considered myself a heavy social media user.

And yet as we come to the halfway point of 2026, the UK faces wide sweeping changes to the social media world. A flat ban for under 16 year olds and, consequently, a requirement to prove that you’re over 16. Given I am not prepared to dish out my government ID to any backwater data broker on the net, I will be using a VPN for as long as that remains an option, and dipping out entirely otherwise. It made me think about how such an important commodity has come to rest in the hands of some of the greediest, most irresponsible people in our society. Big Tech holds the keys to so much of our lives, and our governments have done a historically shit job of protecting us from the greed and irresponsibility of these corporate giants.
title: Why start this site

content:
Forgive the boring title, but that’s exactly what this is. I have the theme up and running and the posts are working as well. Things still to do, questions still to answer:
How do I add those side buttons for archive and tags? Config entry, done How flexible are hugo themes? A little bit, just need to learn how handle bars work Would I be better just writing a static site from scratch? Maybe, I’m out growing the theme very quickly. Do I lose my posts if I do that? Do I have to write my own MD parser??? What about Tags? How do they work? I’m going to add some important pages tonight, the things I started this site for, like “Favourite artists”. I might also write a post explaining some of the more personal reasons I have for starting this site, we’ll see.
summary:
Forgive the boring title, but that’s exactly what this is. I have the theme up and running and the posts are working as well. Things still to do, questions still to answer:

  • How do I add those side buttons for archive and tags?
    • Config entry, done
  • How flexible are hugo themes?
    • A little bit, just need to learn how handle bars work
  • Would I be better just writing a static site from scratch?
    • Maybe, I’m out growing the theme very quickly.
    • Do I lose my posts if I do that? Do I have to write my own MD parser???
  • What about Tags? How do they work?

I’m going to add some important pages tonight, the things I started this site for, like “Favourite artists”. I might also write a post explaining some of the more personal reasons I have for starting this site, we’ll see.

title: Why start this site

content:
Forgive the boring title, but that’s exactly what this is. I have the theme up and running and the posts are working as well. Things still to do, questions still to answer:
How do I add those side buttons for archive and tags? Config entry, done How flexible are hugo themes? A little bit, just need to learn how handle bars work Would I be better just writing a static site from scratch? Maybe, I’m out growing the theme very quickly. Do I lose my posts if I do that? Do I have to write my own MD parser??? What about Tags? How do they work? I’m going to add some important pages tonight, the things I started this site for, like “Favourite artists”. I might also write a post explaining some of the more personal reasons I have for starting this site, we’ll see.
summary:
Forgive the boring title, but that’s exactly what this is. I have the theme up and running and the posts are working as well. Things still to do, questions still to answer:

  • How do I add those side buttons for archive and tags?
    • Config entry, done
  • How flexible are hugo themes?
    • A little bit, just need to learn how handle bars work
  • Would I be better just writing a static site from scratch?
    • Maybe, I’m out growing the theme very quickly.
    • Do I lose my posts if I do that? Do I have to write my own MD parser???
  • What about Tags? How do they work?

I’m going to add some important pages tonight, the things I started this site for, like “Favourite artists”. I might also write a post explaining some of the more personal reasons I have for starting this site, we’ll see.

title: Inspirations

content:
Rats, cats, magpies Trying to define yourself online is a minefield. Even just picking a name feels impossible. Trying to find something that resonates with you is difficult, trying to find something that resonates but isn’t cringy is very difficult, and anything that fits that brief is guaranteed to be taken already. It’s a very annoying horrible venn diagram.
I almost always come back to animals that I identify with. Cats, rats, and birds all appealed to me. Yet all attempts to fit these animals into a name failed miserably (see afformentioned horrible venn diagram). That being said, I feel good with what I’ve settled on. The second line from the magpie nursery rhyme “One for sorrow, Two for joy”. Magpies are definitely in my top 3 birds of all time, and their tendency to pick up shiny things is an accurate parallel to how I pick up new hobbies. I like the idea of a two headed magpie, one head is more reserved, sensible, maybe a bit gloomy. The other head is more eclectic, hopeful, maybe lacking common sense. One head finishes WIPS, the other starts new ones. I’ll try an make a wee logo of them, it’ll be cute.
Yes, inside me are two wolves. Except they’re one magpie. The cringe levels are acceptable thank you.
summary:
Rats, cats, magpies

Trying to define yourself online is a minefield. Even just picking a name feels impossible. Trying to find something that resonates with you is difficult, trying to find something that resonates but isn’t cringy is very difficult, and anything that fits that brief is guaranteed to be taken already. It’s a very annoying horrible venn diagram.
I almost always come back to animals that I identify with. Cats, rats, and birds all appealed to me. Yet all attempts to fit these animals into a name failed miserably (see afformentioned horrible venn diagram). That being said, I feel good with what I’ve settled on. The second line from the magpie nursery rhyme “One for sorrow, Two for joy”. Magpies are definitely in my top 3 birds of all time, and their tendency to pick up shiny things is an accurate parallel to how I pick up new hobbies. I like the idea of a two headed magpie, one head is more reserved, sensible, maybe a bit gloomy. The other head is more eclectic, hopeful, maybe lacking common sense. One head finishes WIPS, the other starts new ones. I’ll try an make a wee logo of them, it’ll be cute.
title: Finding an identity for this blog (and me)

content:
Where else can I go? I’ve always found the wider social media landscape a bit intimidating. I was too young for MSN, never warmed up to facebook, snapchat, insta or whatever came after. I liked youtube, reddit, and discord, where I could enguage in hobby communities without wondering if my family would stumble across a comment I’d written. I never considered myself a heavy social media user.
And yet as we come to the halfway point of 2026, the UK faces wide sweeping changes to the social media world. A flat ban for under 16 year olds and, consequently, a requirement to prove that you’re over 16. Given I am not prepared to dish out my government ID to any backwater data broker on the net, I will be using a VPN for as long as that remains an option, and dipping out entirely otherwise. It made me think about how such an important commodity has come to rest in the hands of some of the greediest, most irresponsible people in our society. Big Tech holds the keys to so much of our lives, and our governments have done a historically shit job of protecting us from the greed and irresponsibility of these corporate giants.
So, I refer you to the video that showed me an alternative. As I write this, I’m struck by the barebones nature of the blog so far compared to the examples in the video, but hopefully that will begin to change!
The Indie Web I just missed out on this era of internet. It would never have occurred to me that the web could be traversed, site to site, link by link. But what a magical way to do it! If we want to shake off the taint of Google, of big tech as a whole, this seems like a lovely place to start. And hey maybe I’ll learn something about web dev along the way. God knows that native Android development as a carrer seems dead in the water.
It’s not perfect, I’ll be using GitHub to host, and will still be using sites like reddit, discord and youtube on a daily basis. But, it is a step in what feels like the right direction.
Keeping myself accountable This is straight forward. I have too many hobbies. True many of them intertwine and support each other, but that doesn’t make it any easier to stick to one project, finishing it and moving on. Crazy, impossible, it can’t be done sir. But, if I can use this blog to keep track of what I’m doing maybe it’ll be easier to keep some sort of mommentum, or at least not forget one of my several WIPs.
Supporting the artists I enjoy If nothing else, I hope someone else might stumble across this and appricate the links to other artists sites. These people amaze me.
summary:
Where else can I go?

I liked youtube, reddit, and discord, where I could enguage in hobby communities without wondering if my family would stumble across a comment I’d written. I never considered myself a heavy social media user.

And yet as we come to the halfway point of 2026, the UK faces wide sweeping changes to the social media world. A flat ban for under 16 year olds and, consequently, a requirement to prove that you’re over 16. Given I am not prepared to dish out my government ID to any backwater data broker on the net, I will be using a VPN for as long as that remains an option, and dipping out entirely otherwise. It made me think about how such an important commodity has come to rest in the hands of some of the greediest, most irresponsible people in our society. Big Tech holds the keys to so much of our lives, and our governments have done a historically shit job of protecting us from the greed and irresponsibility of these corporate giants.

title: Inspirations

content: I started sketching a mascot / logo kind of thing. There’s lots of magpues in my area so there’s no shortage of reference photos. This cutie has a little white dot next to their eye. I’ve kept the white dot and used the pics I took as a ref for the pose. I thought it would be neat if I could generally fit them into a sort of square shape? Like it might make it easier to create a logo if the dimensions are regular. It almost works, and if you fill the top left corner with an icon it fits nicely (moon / start / sun / ‘TFJ’ could all work).

The second line from the magpie nursery rhyme “One for sorrow, Two for joy”. Magpies are definitely in my top 3 birds of all time, and their tendency to pick up shiny things is an accurate parallel to how I pick up new hobbies. I like the idea of a two headed magpie, one head is more reserved, sensible, maybe a bit gloomy. The other head is more eclectic, hopeful, maybe lacking common sense. One head finishes WIPS, the other starts new ones. I’ll try an make a wee logo of them, it’ll be cute.
title: Finding an identity for this blog (and me)

content:
Where else can I go? I’ve always found the wider social media landscape a bit intimidating. I was too young for MSN, never warmed up to facebook, snapchat, insta or whatever came after. I liked youtube, reddit, and discord, where I could enguage in hobby communities without wondering if my family would stumble across a comment I’d written. I never considered myself a heavy social media user.
And yet as we come to the halfway point of 2026, the UK faces wide sweeping changes to the social media world. A flat ban for under 16 year olds and, consequently, a requirement to prove that you’re over 16. Given I am not prepared to dish out my government ID to any backwater data broker on the net, I will be using a VPN for as long as that remains an option, and dipping out entirely otherwise. It made me think about how such an important commodity has come to rest in the hands of some of the greediest, most irresponsible people in our society. Big Tech holds the keys to so much of our lives, and our governments have done a historically shit job of protecting us from the greed and irresponsibility of these corporate giants.
So, I refer you to the video that showed me an alternative. As I write this, I’m struck by the barebones nature of the blog so far compared to the examples in the video, but hopefully that will begin to change!
The Indie Web I just missed out on this era of internet. It would never have occurred to me that the web could be traversed, site to site, link by link. But what a magical way to do it! If we want to shake off the taint of Google, of big tech as a whole, this seems like a lovely place to start. And hey maybe I’ll learn something about web dev along the way. God knows that native Android development as a carrer seems dead in the water.
It’s not perfect, I’ll be using GitHub to host, and will still be using sites like reddit, discord and youtube on a daily basis. But, it is a step in what feels like the right direction.
Keeping myself accountable This is straight forward. I have too many hobbies. True many of them intertwine and support each other, but that doesn’t make it any easier to stick to one project, finishing it and moving on. Crazy, impossible, it can’t be done sir. But, if I can use this blog to keep track of what I’m doing maybe it’ll be easier to keep some sort of mommentum, or at least not forget one of my several WIPs.
Supporting the artists I enjoy If nothing else, I hope someone else might stumble across this and appricate the links to other artists sites. These people amaze me.
summary:
Where else can I go?

I’ve always found the wider social media landscape a bit intimidating. I was too young for MSN, never warmed up to facebook, snapchat, insta or whatever came after. I liked youtube, reddit, and discord, where I could enguage in hobby communities without wondering if my family would stumble across a comment I’d written. I never considered myself a heavy social media user.

And yet as we come to the halfway point of 2026, the UK faces wide sweeping changes to the social media world. A flat ban for under 16 year olds and, consequently, a requirement to prove that you’re over 16. Given I am not prepared to dish out my government ID to any backwater data broker on the net, I will be using a VPN for as long as that remains an option, and dipping out entirely otherwise. It made me think about how such an important commodity has come to rest in the hands of some of the greediest, most irresponsible people in our society. Big Tech holds the keys to so much of our lives, and our governments have done a historically shit job of protecting us from the greed and irresponsibility of these corporate giants.
title: Why start this site

content:
Forgive the boring title, but that’s exactly what this is. I have the theme up and running and the posts are working as well. Things still to do, questions still to answer:
How do I add those side buttons for archive and tags? Config entry, done How flexible are hugo themes? A little bit, just need to learn how handle bars work Would I be better just writing a static site from scratch? Maybe, I’m out growing the theme very quickly. Do I lose my posts if I do that? Do I have to write my own MD parser??? What about Tags? How do they work? I’m going to add some important pages tonight, the things I started this site for, like “Favourite artists”. I might also write a post explaining some of the more personal reasons I have for starting this site, we’ll see.
summary:
Forgive the boring title, but that’s exactly what this is. I have the theme up and running and the posts are working as well. Things still to do, questions still to answer:

  • How do I add those side buttons for archive and tags?
    • Config entry, done
  • How flexible are hugo themes?
    • A little bit, just need to learn how handle bars work
  • Would I be better just writing a static site from scratch?
    • Maybe, I’m out growing the theme very quickly.
    • Do I lose my posts if I do that? Do I have to write my own MD parser???
  • What about Tags? How do they work?

I’m going to add some important pages tonight, the things I started this site for, like “Favourite artists”. I might also write a post explaining some of the more personal reasons I have for starting this site, we’ll see.
